samples.user_head_400:
We are currently solving the following issue within our repository. Here is the issue text:
--- BEGIN ISSUE ---
Using `read()` instead of `read1()` causes unexpected behavior in PtyProcess

```
In [1]: from ptyprocess import PtyProcess
In [2]: p = PtyProcess.spawn(['bash', '-c', 'echo "hello world"'])
In [3]: p.read(100)
Out[3]: b''
In [4]: p.wait()
Out[4]: 0
```

The command executes successfully
samples.gold_fix_head_300:
diff --git a/ptyprocess/ptyprocess.py b/ptyprocess/ptyprocess.py
index 41eae20..0f05803 100644
--- a/ptyprocess/ptyprocess.py
+++ b/ptyprocess/ptyprocess.py
@@ -515,7 +515,7 @@ class PtyProcess(object):
 
         Can block if there is nothing to read. Raises :exc:`EOFError` if the
         terminal

Package settings values in Profile are not correctly accumulated when updating

Description

Python: 3.10
